section.content: The Black Pearl has three masts -- the Fore, Mizzen, and Main. Each mast has three sails:Course, Topsail, and Topgallant. The Course Sail offers a great amount of power but the Topsail can receive a steadier gust of wind. The Topgallant Sails are utilized for added speed when the breeze is low.

The Fore Topmast Staysail and Flying Jib are larger triangular sails used for steering. Both sails are rigged to the Fore Mast and Bowsprit. The Flying Jib has the strongest steering power, while the Fore Topmast Staysail is made of a heavier material that withstands harsh weather.

This is the Black Pearl's third mast, and it has two sails: the Mizzen Topsail and the Mizzen Topmast Staysail. The Mizzen Topsail helps catch a steady breeze for the ship to sail, while the Mizzen Topmast Staysail keeps the ship on course. Anchors away!

Near the stern - on deck, you'll find the Captain's Quarters, the Belfry, and the Ship's Wheel. The Belfry is a belltower that is rung every hour, signifying the time to keep our pirates on task! The Rudder is controlled from the Ship's Wheel, called the tiller, or the Helm -- from the Germanic word khelman.

The Main Deck gives us the Capstan, the Gun Deck, and the Jolly Boats. The Capstan is used to raise or drop the Main Course Sail by turning it. It works like pressing rewind or fast-forward on a tape recorder (also known as a capstan). On the Port Side lies the Gun Deck, and jolly boats are stored on deck so pirates can get to shore for errands, and pillaging, of course!

The Anchor digs into the seabed, but keeps from being buried -- which is key. No pirate can handle being locked in -- even at sea. Every pirate ship needs an anchor to either stop completely or keep the ship stable. Got it, landlubbers?

The Bowsprit is a mast, even though it lays slightly elevated from the horizon. It anchors the Fore Topmast Staysail and Flying Jib, making the Fore Mast closer to the bow of the ship and further away from the Main Mast, creating a much-needed breeze to get our ship on the go.

This is the largest room on our ship, occupied by the one and only Captain Jack Sparrow. When the ship is under attack, this room can be transformed into a surgery due to its spaciousness amongst very cramped quarters.
The crew sleeps in the hammocks that line the sides of the ship. Underneath and sometime to the sides of the hammocks are more guns from the Black Pearl's arsenal if the need arises.

A Figurehead is a carved decoration at the prow of a ship, beginning in the 16th century -- often displaying the name of a ship. Originally, the Black Pearl was a merchant vessel under the employment of the East India Trading Company named the Wicked Wench, helmed by Captain Jack. After refusing to complete an assignment, Lord Cutler Beckett sanctioned the torching (and sinking) of the Wicked Wench, and branded Jack Sparrow a pirate. Jack petitioned Davy Jones to retrieve his ship that he rechristened as: 'The Black Pearl.'

The lowest part of the ship is full of supplies, cargo, ammunition, food, dead weight, and bottles of Captain Jack's rum, of course. Ships are designed to float above the water but must be weighed down to submerge enough of the ship's hull -- a real balancing act.
